meaning: n.吹口哨的人,啸啸作声的东西
examples: She told Whistler that his portrait of her was finished and to stop fiddling with it.
A good whistler can be heard and understood five miles away.
Anyone who has heard a top whistler will agree that this record is quite possible.
The report also cited the strong quarterly dividend history at Whistler Blackcomb Holdings Inc.
Last spring, we trekked two hours north of Vancouver to the bustling ski town of Whistler.
